The odd bloom of woodland really stands out, which is why, a couple hours out from New T’s ranch, the much coveted waterhole is not difficult to spot. You set the hovercab down with a wheeze at the edge of the trees: hybrid Terran oaks, willows and beeches, regal old growth. There’s a wonderfully cool balm to be found underneath their boughs, aided by the large, rocky pool you find nestled near the middle. It’s fed by a spring with a stream chattering away into the undergrowth off to one side. It appears completely deserted, although the existence of a tire swing on the steepest bank tells you that the locals are aware of it. Sunlight glints off the surface, and avian life twitters and sings in the branches. It might be heaven. The girls ferry their stuff from the hovercab trunk, pale yellow dresses swishing, chattering frenetically the whole time. In due course there are towels, a big cooler of food and drink, a little floating audio drone, a beachball, sunscreen, and holo devices arrayed on the gravelly shore. They shuck off their billowy dresses, revealing their two-piece bikinis — Carrie’s is a light blue, Cora’s is crimson, both barely up to the task of containing their milky, quivering curves — and dip their toes in the water with twitching coos and giggles. “Isn’t this wonderful, big [pc.guy]?” Carrie turns a big grin on you. “C’mon! Let’s have some fun!” You put on your [pc.underGarments] before slipping into the cool water, and it is indeed wonderful against the hot, dry heat of New Texan air. You grow accustomed to the chill and splash around with the pair of them for a while, exulting in being all alone in this lush spot. After a bit, though, you decide to diversify your fun.

It’s so worth it, though.] The twins giggle and cheer the first time you soak them with one of your cannonballs. The third and fourth time, not so much. “Stooop it,” Carrie whines, splashing at you when you emerge fountaining cool water again. Ok, you probably should... but then the swing catches your eye again, like the seductive minx it is. You can’t help but think you haven’t experienced it to its fullest potential. Like, if you charged at it flat out, and let go right at the apex of the swing, you would be flung right over the two girls paddling around beneath, not catching them with any spray at all (probably)! Generously and exuberantly. Obviously you know what those look like, but there’s something specifically about the Treated which make them impossible not to ogle. You can’t help your eyes being drawn to those mountains of jiggling, creamy flesh, cold water keeping their plump nipples hard beneath the thin, shifting fabric of their bikinis, strings heroically taut against the heavy, supple weight of... ...the ball bops you on the forehead. High-pitched giggling rings around the clearing. “What’s the matter, big [pc.boy]? It’s a simple game! Can’t you concentrate?” “[pc.His] tongue is practically danglin’ out!” “Aww. Don’t be mean to [pc.him], sis.” Carrie pulls herself to her sister’s side, smiling at you as she presses her boob into Cora’s shoulder, plumping the pale softness outwards. “[pc.He] can’t help keepin’ [pc.his] eyes on what really matters, can [pc.he]?” You don’t think they minded your eyes drifting much.

original: Putting the homemade food to one side is tough, but weighed against the equally sensual enjoyment of sun-warmed cowgirl, it’s not so bad. You wash your hands, scoot over to where Cora is lying, and tell her she needs some more sunscreen. “No I don’t,” she murmurs. “But... ooh,” she sighs, as you rub your lotion-slathered hands together and then apply them to her smooth, hot back. “Ummm... yes, you can keep doing that.” You don’t need to be any kind of experienced masseuse to make a cowgirl purr; you just need the tiniest amount of patience and to be reasonably gentle. It only takes a few sweeps of your oily hands up the curve of Cora’s back, neck, and upper arms, feeling her slender muscles under her soft, supple flesh, to make her fall into something like a sensual coma, groaning, sighing and then mooing quietly, auburn tail-puff flicking dreamily. You let your fingers roam a little more, pulling her swimsuit down a bit so the swell of her juicy backside can receive some much needed manual attention — and no complaint is registered from you doing so. “Do me next!” Carrie says brightly, laying herself down beside her sister. “Moooo,” she’s groaning about a minute later as you varnish her pale skin to a shine. “Around my shoulders... oh that’s it... moooo!” “ -nk you f’r leavin’ s-m f’r mme,” Cora chews, a little later still. The dilated pupils and goofy, happy grins the pair of them are turning on you now say thank you much clearer. Such tactile creatures.

But you appear even bigger when you raise your arms and yell at it as loud as you can, charging out of the water towards it like an outraged hippopotamus as you do. Go on, git!” “Yeah! Wooowww! Our hero!” You are presently sandwiched between two cooing cowgirls, looking up at you with wide, worshipful eyes as they shower you with praise. Your [pc.chest] swells; you slide your arms around their waists and give them a joint, comforting squeeze. It was nothing, really... you just have to show these animals who’s the boss. “Well, that’s you alright!” Carrie bats her lashes at you and leans forward, so you can get a gigantic eyeful of her cleavage. “The boss.” You are handfed the little yellow fruit in the relieved, idyllic picnic that follows. You and the cowgirls are forced to watch as it helps itself, ignoring the carbon-based chicken in favor of the fruit, pie, and salad, before eating the cooler itself. Only then does it mosey off. “You were supposed to scare ‘em off!” You’re feeling beat enough without having to hear this too, but hear it you must. You slump down on your towel as the twins march back down the beach to berate you. “It took me half a day to make all that food!” “What use is a big [pc.guy] if [pc.he] can’t even shoo a titchy lil varmint off?!” “WE’RE havin’ the chicken. If you want somethin’ to eat, you kin go hunt that thing down like a real bull would!” This hardly seems fair, but you suppose you have to accept the downsides of the machismic role you’ve adopted around these two, and you go hungry with a show of stoicism. At least after they’ve feasted on the drumsticks Carrie and Cora are a little more content. ]

The girls are not happy. “You were supposed to scare ‘em off!” “It took me half a day to make all that food!” “What use is a big [pc.guy] if [pc.he] can’t even shoo a titchy lil varmint off?!” You roll your eyes at this barrage of criticism and tell them it was far safer to just let the creature have its way than to try and fend it off with no weapons. Do either of them have first aid training? Well then. “Well...” Carrie is still fuming a little. “...I still think a real bull would’ve at least tried.” Taking charge of the situation in some way helped a bit you think, and after they’ve had what remains, they mellowed out some.
